Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'W', 'B'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"id={n['id']} color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} center=({sum(rows)//len(rows)},{sum(cols)//len(cols)})")

# Check the right frame area
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nRight frame area:")
for i in range(24, 32):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {lines[i][40:52]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
